text: Git branching is an essential part of modern version control. When working on a project, developers create feature branches to build new features without affecting the main codebase. This allows multiple developers to work on different tasks at the same time. Once a feature is complete, it can be merged back into the main branch. However, before merging, the team usually opens a pull request. This is a request for others to review the code before it is added. The pull request allows discussion, comments, and improvements. This workflow helps teams collaborate effectively, keep the main branch stable, and track all changes to the project.
